name: Stuffed Baked Mussels
recipeIngredient: 3 pounds Prince Edward Island mussels, about 50 to 60
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450 degrees.
2. Clean and debeard the mussels. Place the mussels in a 12- to 14-inch sauté pan or large saucepan with the wine, cover, and place over high heat to steam. The mussels should steam open in 2 to 3 minutes.
3. Drain the mussels, reserving the liquid. Place the liquid in a medium saucepan and add the basic tomato sauce. Bring to boil, lower the heat to simmer, and reduce to 2 cups. Allow to cool.
4. Open the mussels and remove the piece of shell that the mussel has detached itself from. Loosen the mussel from the second shell but leave it intact in the shell.
5. Lay the mussels on a large baking sheet. Spoon 1/2 teaspoon of tomato sauce mixture onto each mussel. Chiffonade all basil leaves and place several ribbons of basil over each mussel.
6. Sprinkle with half a teaspoon of bread crumbs and dot with 2 to 3 drops of olive oil. Bake for 4 to 5 minutes, or until hot. Serve immediately with lemon wedges.
